title: casselia lanceolata
intro: Casselia lanceolata, also known as Mexican Silverleaf or Desert Lavender, is a plant species in the family Boraginaceae. It is a shrubby perennial with silvery-gray leaves and small purple flowers, native to Mexico and parts of the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce inflammation. Casselia lanceolata can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues, as well as to promote skin health and relieve pain.
